tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Repost campaign â€” company creation, frequency, user display
tasks.description: Full repost campaign feature: companies provide exact pre-written post content per platform instead of AI generation. Formats: text only, image only, text+image. Posting frequency: once, daily, every X days, weekly (stored as repost_frequency on Campaign model). Company dashboard: create form with per-platform text + image URL fields, campaign detail shows repost content card with edit support (POST /campaigns/{id}/repost-content), frequency selector. User dashboard: repost drafts shown as read-only with banner, no edit/regenerate, approve/reject only. Background agent: skips 4-phase AI pipeline for reposts, uses CampaignPost records directly, respects frequency setting, converts Reddit title+body format. Bugs already fixed: field name mismatch (campaign_posts vs repost_content) in local_db.py and background_agent.py, Reddit format conversion. Quality gate: image-only posts valid, content_guidance not required. Content rotation (multiple posts per platform) is a future sub-feature.

tasks.title: E2E deleted post detection â€” full pipeline verification
tasks.description: Verify the full deleted post pipeline on ALL 4 platforms (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit): scraper visits deleted URL, detects deletion, marks post deleted locally, notifies server via PATCH /api/posts/{id}/status, server voids pending earnings, funds return to campaign budget. Must test each platform individually. Scraper detection was tested in Task #6 but the full server notification + earning voiding E2E flow was not.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] UGC-style content â€” make AI content look authentic and drive viral views
tasks.description: Make AI-generated content look like authentic user-generated content that drives viral views. Content should not look AI-written or corporate. Posts must reference the company's actual product/service using latest trends, news, and product updates â€” this makes posts accurate and feel like real UGC from someone who actually uses the product. Includes: platform-native writing styles (casual X threads, story-format LinkedIn, community-tone Reddit), anti-AI detection (no AI buzzwords, varied sentence structure, imperfect human touches), engagement-optimized hooks (contrarian takes, curiosity gaps, emotional triggers tied to current events and product features), visual UGC aesthetic for images (phone-quality look, natural lighting, handwritten annotations vs polished graphics). Goal: content that gets shared because it feels real, not because it looks like an ad.

tasks.title: Fully disable X across Amplifier — hardcoded safety guard
tasks.description: X's anti-bot detection has suspended the user's accounts 3 times total (original X account permanently locked predating Amplifier; 2026-03-24 replacement account first block during E2E testing; 2026-04-14 replacement account suspended during test posting from Amplifier). config/platforms.json already has enabled=false but code paths still reference X. This task: (1) audit every X/twitter reference across scripts/, config/, server/, docs/, (2) ensure posting engine, profile scraping, metric scraping, content generation, matching, login_setup, and background agent ALL skip X unconditionally via a HARDCODED guard — not just the config flag, (3) surface the block in user-app UI with tooltip explaining the risk, (4) preserve code (don't delete) so we can re-enable later with a safe method (X API v2, stealth browser like camoufox, or equivalent). Acceptance: setting X enabled=true in config must NOT re-enable posting/scraping; safety guard in code must refuse. All X UI in settings shows disabled state with tooltip. Priority: trust-critical before user onboarding — a suspended user is a product-killer.

metadata.reason: safety-critical; 3 X suspensions total across 2 accounts

tasks.title: Plan + execute Vercel migration (target host undecided)
tasks.description: Server offline after Vercel billed ~$47/cycle (serverless model incompatible with Amplifier's high poll volume from user apps). Project deleted, billing disputed. Migration path NOT YET DECIDED — still in planning. Full planning doc with analysis, options, and step-by-step plan: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md. Leading candidate is shared Hostinger KVM VPS (owned by Nili+Daniel, co-hosts TTE+Stock Buddy) but not confirmed. Fallback options include Render flat-fee and Railway.
tasks.details:
READ FIRST: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md — 12 sections covering options analysis, server requirements, step-by-step setup, env vars, client cutover, Vercel cleanup (~62 files), cutover sequence, rollback, cost comparison, follow-ups, and open questions. Before executing: (1) decide target host (Hostinger VPS vs Render vs Railway vs other), (2) confirm with Nili/Daniel if going Hostinger route — KVM tier, SSH access, existing Caddy/Redis/UFW, subdomain, (3) audit ENCRYPTION_KEY usage before rotating, (4) verify ARQ WorkerSettings entrypoint exists. Clients still pointing to dead Vercel URL via config/.env:7 and scripts/utils/server_client.py:22. Server is OFFLINE right now — users can't access anything until this ships.

=== HOSTING DECISION (added 2026-04-25 after father review) ===
Three viable options compared. Recommended: Option B (existing shared Hostinger VPS).

Option A — New free Vercel account: REJECTED. Same trap. Serverless billing scales with traffic;
  Amplifier's 10-min poll loop from every user app instance will burn the free tier quickly and
  generate the same surprise charge that killed the original project ($47/cycle).

Option B — Existing Hostinger KVM VPS (shared with TTE + Stock Buddy, owned by Daniel + Nili):
  RECOMMENDED. Flat fee already paid (sunk cost). Has Caddy + Redis + UFW configured. Supports
  systemd + ARQ workers cleanly. Requires SSH access handoff from Nili. Risk: shared resource
  contention with TTE (1300+ symbols scaling to 14k) and Stock Buddy. If contention becomes an
  issue, fall back to Option C.

Option C — Second Hostinger account (separate VPS dedicated to Amplifier): FALLBACK. Pros: no
  shared-resource concern, full control. Cons: extra ongoing cost, extra ops surface. Use only if
  Amplifier outgrows shared resources or Daniel/Nili want isolation.

DECISION ORDER:
  1. Start with Option B. Get SSH access from Nili. Deploy via the migration steps already in
     docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md. ~2-3 hours of work.
  2. Monitor resource usage for first 4 weeks. If stable, stay on B.
  3. If contention shows up (CPU steal, DB slowdowns from neighbors), migrate to Option C.

Option A (new free Vercel) is OFF the table — same failure mode.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Re-enable TikTok, Instagram, and X (cheap API path)
tasks.description: Re-enable the three currently-disabled platforms so creators have full 6-platform reach. TikTok and Instagram use static test videos/images (no AI video generation in this task). X re-enables only via the official paid API v2 — never resume browser automation on X.
tasks.details:
CONTEXT — current state:
- X: hardcoded-disabled via scripts/utils/guard.py (Task #40 done) after 3 X account suspensions
  caused by anti-bot detection. The user's father has indicated the X API is now cheaper than
  before; verify the actual pricing of X API v2 'Basic' tier before committing budget.
- TikTok: enabled=false in config/platforms.json. Code paths in scripts/post.py + config/scripts/
  exist but are not actively maintained. TikTok requires VPN to non-India server (Surfshark or
  equivalent) because it is geo-blocked from India. Document VPN setup in onboarding.
- Instagram: enabled=false in config/platforms.json. Multi-step posting flow (Create → Post →
  Upload → Next → Next → Caption → Share) preserved but not exercised in months. All clicks need
  force=True due to overlay intercepts.

SCOPE — three sub-tasks, each independently testable:

1. X API integration (re-enable via official API only):
   - Research X API v2 pricing as of implementation date. 'Basic' tier ($100/mo) allows up to
     10K writes/month — enough for many users. 'Free' tier is read-only.
   - If pricing acceptable: integrate via tweepy or direct httpx calls. Token stored encrypted
     locally per-user (each user provides their own X API key in onboarding).
   - Remove the hardcoded guard in scripts/utils/guard.py and server/app/utils/platform_guard.py.
     Replace with a soft 'X API not configured' fallback so users without an X key just skip X.
   - DO NOT re-enable browser automation on X under any circumstance — only the API path.
   - Test on a fresh disposable X developer account before rolling out to existing users.

2. TikTok (with test videos):
   - Restore the existing posting flow in config/scripts/ (TikTok posting script).
   - Onboarding addition: detect if user is in a geo-blocked region (e.g., India) and prompt
     them to configure a VPN before connecting TikTok.
   - For this task ONLY use static test videos from a local folder. NO AI video generation;
     that is Task #31 (deferred separately).
   - Verify TikTok still detects the Draft.js editor reliably (Ctrl+A → Backspace clear before typing).

3. Instagram (images + test videos):
   - Restore the multi-step dialog flow. Test images first; videos require Instagram's separate
     Reel flow which is fragile.
   - All buttons need force=True due to overlay intercepts — preserve this.
   - Image upload via 'Photo/video' button + hidden file input.

INTEGRATION POINTS:
- All three platforms must show in the user app's Connect Platforms wizard
- Matching engine must include them as eligible target platforms
- Billing must support per-platform payout rates for all three
- Profile scraping must work on all three (TikTok/IG already have stubs)
tasks.testStrategy:
Per-platform smoke test:
1. TikTok: connect on a VPN-enabled machine, post a 5-second test video, capture URL, scrape metrics 24h later. Verify metrics arrive.
2. Instagram: connect, upload a test image with caption, capture URL, scrape metrics. Verify the permalink resolves to the actual post.
3. X (API path): with a developer key configured, post a tweet via the API, capture URL, scrape metrics via the same API. Verify the user's X account is NOT suspended (key signal of API-vs-automation success).
Acceptance: all three platforms post a test piece of content and metrics flow back to the server. X re-enable can be split into a separate sub-task if API cost remains prohibitive.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Shared research pool — campaigns share research across users
tasks.description: When the 4-phase content agent does research for a campaign on one user's machine, push that research to the server so other users assigned to the same campaign can read it instead of re-doing the work. Forms a collective research pool per campaign that all assigned users contribute to and consume from.
tasks.details:
CONTEXT — current behaviour (Task #14, shipped):
- The content agent's Phase 1 (Research) runs on the user's machine. It scrapes the company's URLs
  (up to 3), reads campaign images via vision AI, searches the web for recent niche news, and
  synthesizes a 'research context' (product summary, key features, content angles, emotional
  hooks, recent news headlines, etc.).
- This research is cached LOCALLY for 7 days (per scripts/utils/content_generator.py).
- Every other user on the same campaign re-does the exact same work on their own machine.
- Wasteful: identical Gemini calls, identical web scrapes, identical token cost.

GOAL — turn it into a shared pool:
1. After research finishes locally, agent uploads the research blob to the server keyed by
   (campaign_id, research_version). Server stores it on a new CampaignResearch table.
2. Before doing research locally, agent first asks server: 'do you have research for campaign X
   that is < 7 days old?'. If yes, downloads and caches locally. If partial (e.g., URLs scraped
   but no news search done yet), agent does only the missing parts and uploads the delta.
3. Research is split into discrete sections (product_scrape, image_analysis, niche_news,
   emotional_hooks, content_angles). Each section is independently versionable so partial
   research is mergeable.
4. Server merges contributions from multiple users — last-write-wins per section, but the union
   of all sections forms the final cached pool.

SCHEMA additions (new model):
- CampaignResearch: id, campaign_id (FK), section ('product_scrape' | 'image_analysis' |
  'niche_news' | 'emotional_hooks' | 'content_angles'), payload (JSON), contributed_by_user_id,
  created_at, expires_at
- Index: (campaign_id, section, created_at desc) for fast 'latest fresh research' lookup

API additions (new endpoints):
- GET  /api/campaigns/{id}/research → returns latest non-expired sections, merged
- POST /api/campaigns/{id}/research → upload a section payload

AGENT changes (scripts/utils/content_generator.py):
- Before each research section: GET from server, use if fresh
- After each research section: POST to server with payload + section name
- Local 7-day cache becomes a fallback if server is unreachable

BENEFITS:
- Token cost per campaign drops from O(users × research) to O(research)
- Faster onboarding for late-joiners — research already cached
- Companies could optionally see the research pool (could become a 'transparency' feature later)

EDGE CASES:
- Race conditions: two users research the same section at the same time → last-write-wins is fine
- Stale research: 7-day TTL expires → next user redoes it; subsequent users benefit again
- Privacy: nothing user-identifying in research payloads (just product/market info), safe to share
- Trust: a malicious user could inject bad research. Mitigation: server-side AI can validate or
  flag obviously-junk research before caching it (post-MVP refinement)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Two users (User A, User B) accept the same campaign within minutes of each other.
2. User A's agent runs research first → uploads 5 sections to server.
3. User B's agent starts research second → expect server returns existing sections, B does NO
   Gemini calls, downloads research, generates content using A's research.
4. User C joins same campaign 5 days later → same behaviour as B.
5. User D joins 8 days later → research is expired, D redoes research and re-uploads.
6. Subsequent users in the same 7-day window after D benefit from D's fresh research.
Verify: token spend dropped on B/C/D's machines. Server logs show GET hit + POST contributions.

tasks.title: Implement ARQ worker entrypoint
tasks.description: Define app.worker.WorkerSettings so background jobs auto-run: earning promotion (7-day hold), trust-score sweeps, billing reconciliation. Currently codebase imports arq>=0.26.1 but no WorkerSettings class exists. BLOCKING before Task #17 (Stripe live + paying users) — without this, paying users' pending earnings never auto-promote to available.

tasks.title: Generate baseline Alembic migration + enforce migrations going forward
tasks.description: Discovered during Task #41 post-deploy UAT: server/alembic/ is configured but server/alembic/versions/ is EMPTY. No migrations have ever been generated. Models drifted silently from prod schema, causing 2 prod-blocking bugs (decline_reason missing, 14 json columns needed jsonb). Both manually fixed via psql; SQL captured at docs/migrations/2026-04-25-task41-schema-fixes.md. WORK: (1) Generate baseline alembic revision, (2) stamp prod, (3) document policy in CLAUDE.md that every model change needs migration in same PR. PRIORITY: Medium — not blocking deploy or current functionality, BLOCKING before Task #15 (AI quality gate may add columns).
